mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in residential housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks colliding with shifting consumer preferences, which compresses corporate margins and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances where both proxies move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, an increase in real savings deposits signals flight-to-safety behavior that drains equity liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when FEDFUNDS accelerates and RSAFS expands simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter mechanically lifts corporate revenue expectations and earnings multiples, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in GDP expansion quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 295.0, it confirms entrenched inflationary pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the CPI threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining personal savings indicates a liquidity crunch that forces retail deleveraging and equity liquidation, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances where both proxies move in the specified directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion phase, a divergence where industrial production falls while housing starts rise signals a rotation from cyclical manufacturing to defensive real estate, which reduces broad market momentum and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when the specified production-housing divergence occurs, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.
